 {"id":"bd-39vx.5","title":"Modal Focus and Accessibility","description":"Focus management and ARIA-like semantics for modals.

## Requirements
- Auto-focus first focusable in modal
- Focus trap within modal bounds
- Restore focus on close
- Escape key to close

## Implementation
- Integrate with FocusManager
- Store pre-modal focus state
- aria-modal semantic marker

## Success Criteria
- Tab cannot escape modal
- Focus returns to trigger on close
- Escape closes modal

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Modal Focus and Accessibility
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-39vx.6","title":"Modal/Dialog E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of modal and dialog functionality.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Basic Modal (modal_basic_e2e.rs)
- Open modal, verify rendering
- Close via backdrop click
- Close via Escape key
- Close via close button

### 2. Dialog Variants (modal_dialog_e2e.rs)
- Alert dialog with single button
- Confirm dialog with OK/Cancel
- Prompt dialog with input
- Custom dialog content

### 3. Modal Stack (modal_stack_e2e.rs)
- Open nested modals
- Z-order correctness
- Close order (LIFO)
- Input isolation to top modal

### 4. Focus Management (modal_focus_e2e.rs)
- Auto-focus first element
- Focus trap within modal
- Tab cycles correctly
- Focus restore on close

### 5. Animation (modal_animation_e2e.rs)
- Open animation frames
- Close animation frames
- Backdrop fade timing
- Animation interruption

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Modal lifecycle (open, close, focus)
- DEBUG: Z-index calculations
- TRACE: Focus trap events

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/modal_dialog/

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Modal/Dialog E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-39y4","title":"Demo Showcase: Command Palette + Instant Action Search","description":"## Background
Command Palette is the fastest way to discover and trigger actions. This demo should feel instant, predictable, and polished, and act as the canonical reference implementation for FrankenTUI apps.

## Scope / Goals
- Typeahead fuzzy search over action registry
- Stable scoring and ordering; no "jumpiness"
- Keyboard-only navigation and execution
- Optional preview panel with action details
- Works in both inline + alt-screen modes
- Zero external dependencies unless strictly necessary

## Non‑Goals
- No network calls or remote indexing
- No persistence of history beyond in‑memory (unless explicitly added later)

## Deliverables
- Palette widget + matcher + ranking utilities
- Demo showcase integration (overlay + preview)
- Test suite: unit, snapshot, perf, PTY E2E
- Docs + demo script

## Testing & Logging (Must-Haves)
- Unit tests for matcher/ranking + selection logic
- Snapshot tests for empty/results/selection/preview states
- Perf regression tests with fixed datasets (1k/5k actions)
- PTY E2E tests with verbose logs including query latency and result counts
- Feature-gated telemetry hooks for palette open/close, query updates, execution

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fuzzy search returns deterministic, stable ranking
- [ ] Keyboard navigation is consistent and documented
- [ ] Preview panel renders correctly without flicker
- [ ] p95 query latency logged and under agreed budget in demo dataset
- [ ] Unit + snapshot + perf + E2E tests all pass
- [ ] Docs + demo script included

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Demo Showcase: Command Palette + Instant Action Search
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.
- Color: operate in perceptual space (OKLCH/CIELAB) with hard contrast constraints; precompute palettes via constrained optimization and prove WCAG bounds in tests.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:28:38.508327808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:13:07.911632559Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","polish","search","ux","widgets"],"comments":[{"id":151,"issue_id":"bd-39y4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background
- Users want fast, discoverable actions (e.g., /dp/coding_agent_session_search). FrankenTUI should showcase a first‑class command palette implemented purely in ftui.

Goals
- Typeahead search over actions with fast fuzzy matching and stable scoring.
- Instant keyboard navigation and execution (Enter/Tab) with clear previews.
- Demonstrate in demo showcase with zero external dependencies.

Non-Goals
- No network access; search index is in‑memory.

Testing
- Unit tests for matcher/scoring and selection behavior.
- PTY E2E tests that simulate typing, navigation, and execution with verbose logging.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:29:32Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-39y4.1","title":"Command Palette spec + UX flow","description":"## Purpose
Define the UX and behavioral spec for the command palette so implementation and tests are unambiguous.

## UX / Behavior Spec
- Open/close: `Ctrl+P` (primary), `/` optional if not used by search elsewhere; `Esc` closes.
- Navigation: Up/Down, PageUp/PageDown, Home/End; `Enter` executes; `Tab` toggles preview focus (if present).
- Query input: live filtering; backspace deletes; empty query shows top actions.
- Empty state: show “No results” + hints for query syntax.
- Selection: always visible highlight + optional right-side preview.
- Action metadata: title, optional description, category, and tags.

## Scoring Rules (must be deterministic)
- Prefer contiguous matches over scattered
- Prefer start‑of‑word matches
- Prefer shorter candidates given equal match quality
- Stable tie‑breakers (original index) to avoid reordering flicker

## Accessibility
- Color is not the only indicator; selection should invert or add marker.
- Min contrast for selection/highlight.

## Tests (Required)
- Unit tests for scoring rules, stable ordering, and edge cases
- Snapshot tests for empty/results/preview states
- PTY E2E for open→type→navigate→execute (verbose logs)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Spec covers keybindings, scoring, empty state, and preview behavior
- [ ] Stable deterministic ranking defined
- [ ] Accessibility considerations documented
- [ ] Test matrix included

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.
","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- UX spec covers triggers, keyboard flow, empty states, and accessibility notes.
- Includes unit-test plan for keybinding resolution and focus management.
- Includes E2E test plan with scripted keystrokes and structured logging (query, candidate count, selected action).
- Logs specify timing and outcome fields for later perf/UX analysis.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:29:41.984469495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:13:44.595802424Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","design","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39y4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-39y4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:29:41.984469495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-39y4.10","title":"Command Palette — Accessibility/UX Review","description":"## Accessibility / UX Review Checklist

- Keyboard-only flow works end‑to‑end
- Selection state visible without color reliance
- Contrast of highlight meets WCAG AA for text
- Long titles truncate gracefully with ellipsis
- Help overlay lists palette keybindings

## Tests
- Snapshot showing high-contrast selection style
- PTY E2E: navigate with keyboard only and execute

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All checklist items pass
- [ ] No focus traps or unreachable actions

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-39y4.11","title":"Command Palette: Bayesian Match Scoring + Evidence Ledger","description":"Define probabilistic scoring model with Bayes factors and evidence ledger for match explanations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Command Palette: Bayesian Match Scoring + Evidence Ledger
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Math spec: define the model, priors/assumptions, and failure modes; include a reproducible notebook-like derivation in comments/docs
- Guarantees: prefer anytime-valid testing (e-process) or distribution-free calibration (conformal) where applicable

- Guarantees: prefer anytime-valid testing (e-process) or distribution-free calibration (conformal) where applicable
